examples: It was isolated from Streptococcus agalactiae from 17.
Pneumococcal disease is caused by infection with the Streptococcus pneumoniae bacterium.
Nisin is a natural food preservative produced by certain strains of Streptococcus lactis.
It also attacked Streptococcus mutans, an acid-producing bacterium which is a major cause of tooth decay.
Previous research found that enzyme-modified milk could stop Streptococcus mutans from binding to tooth enamel.
